# pfsense_system_advanced_misc (Data Source)

Retrieves the [system advanced miscellaneous](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/config/advanced/miscellaneous.html) configuration including proxy settings, load balancing, power savings, cryptographic hardware, security mitigations, gateway monitoring, and RAM disk settings.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "pfsense_system_advanced_misc" "example" {}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `captive_portal_backup_interval` (Number) Captive portal data backup interval in hours (0-24). Set to 0 to disable periodic captive portal backup. Only applicable when use_mfs_tmpvar is true.
- `crypto_hardware` (String) Cryptographic hardware acceleration module. Options: ``, `aesni`, `cryptodev`, `aesni_cryptodev`. Empty string means no acceleration.
- `dhcp_backup_interval` (Number) DHCP leases backup interval in hours (0-24). Set to 0 to disable periodic DHCP backup. Only applicable when use_mfs_tmpvar is true.
- `do_not_send_unique_id` (Boolean) Do not send the pfSense unique identifier with crash reports and update checks.
- `dpinger_dont_add_static_routes` (Boolean) Do not add static routes for gateway monitor addresses. By default, static routes are added to ensure gateway monitoring traffic uses the correct interface.
- `gw_down_kill_states` (String) Flush states when a gateway goes down. Options: ``, `none`, `down`, `all`. Empty string means no action.
- `hard_disk_standby` (String) Hard disk standby time. Options: ``, `6`, `12`, `24`, `36`, `48`, `60`, `90`, `120`, `180`, `240`, `241`, `242`. Empty string means 'Always On' (no standby).
- `hwpstate` (String) Intel Speed Shift Technology (HWP) state. This field is hardware-dependent and may not be available on all systems. Values are 'enabled' or 'disabled', empty if unsupported.
- `hwpstate_control_level` (String) Intel Speed Shift control level. '0' for per-core control, '1' for per-package control. Only applicable when hwpstate is 'enabled'.
- `hwpstate_epp` (Number) Intel Speed Shift Energy/Performance Preference (0-100). 0 is maximum performance, 100 is maximum energy savings. -1 indicates unsupported/not configured.
- `lb_use_sticky` (Boolean) Use sticky connections for load balancer. Successive connections from the same source will be redirected to the same web server.
- `logs_backup_interval` (Number) Log files backup interval in hours (0-24). Set to 0 to disable periodic log backup. Only applicable when use_mfs_tmpvar is true.
- `mds_disable` (String) Microarchitectural Data Sampling (MDS) mitigation mode. Options: ``, `0`, `1`, `2`, `3`. Empty string uses system default.
- `php_memory_limit` (Number) PHP memory limit in MiB. Set to 0 for the system default. Higher values may be needed for large configurations.
- `powerd_ac_mode` (String) PowerD mode when running on AC power. Options: `hadp`, `adp`, `min`, `max`. Defaults to `hadp`.
- `powerd_battery_mode` (String) PowerD mode when running on battery power. Options: `hadp`, `adp`, `min`, `max`. Defaults to `hadp`.
- `powerd_enable` (Boolean) Enable the powerd utility to manage CPU frequency dynamically based on system load.
- `powerd_normal_mode` (String) PowerD mode when power source is unknown. Options: `hadp`, `adp`, `min`, `max`. Defaults to `hadp`.
- `proxy_pass` (String, Sensitive) Proxy authentication password. Only applicable when proxy_url and proxy_user are set.
- `proxy_port` (Number) Proxy port number. Only applicable when proxy_url is set.
- `proxy_url` (String) Proxy URL or hostname for the system to use when making outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests (e.g., for package manager, update checks).
- `proxy_user` (String) Proxy authentication username. Only applicable when proxy_url is set.
- `pti_disabled` (Boolean) Disable kernel Page Table Isolation (PTI). PTI is a mitigation for Meltdown (CVE-2017-5754). Disabling may improve performance but reduces security.
- `rrd_backup_interval` (Number) RRD data backup interval in hours (0-24). Set to 0 to disable periodic RRD backup. Only applicable when use_mfs_tmpvar is true.
- `schedule_states` (Boolean) Do not kill connections when a schedule expires. By default, when a schedule expires, connections permitted by that schedule are killed.
- `skip_rules_gw_down` (Boolean) Do not create rules when gateway is down. By default, when a rule has a specific gateway, the rule is created and traffic is sent to the default gateway. This option overrides that behavior.
- `src_track` (Number) Source tracking timeout in seconds for sticky connections. Set to 0 for the default. Only applicable when lb_use_sticky is true.
- `thermal_hardware` (String) Thermal sensor hardware module. Options: ``, `coretemp`, `amdtemp`. Empty string means no thermal sensor.
- `use_mfs_tmp_size` (Number) Size of the /tmp RAM disk in MiB. Set to 0 for the default (40 MiB). Only applicable when use_mfs_tmpvar is true.
- `use_mfs_tmpvar` (Boolean) Use RAM disks for /tmp and /var. Useful for embedded systems or to reduce disk writes.
- `use_mfs_var_size` (Number) Size of the /var RAM disk in MiB. Set to 0 for the default (60 MiB). Only applicable when use_mfs_tmpvar is true.
